senses.gloss: shrine, holy burial place associated with a religious figure
maqam (Arabic musical scale), tonality, mode, key
noun of place of أَقَامَ (ʔaqāma): residence

examples.text: يَا أَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لَا مُقَامَ لَكُمْ بِهَا ... قُتِلَ الْحُسَيْنُ فَأَدْمُعِي مِدْرَارُ
examples.english: O people of Yathrib (i.e. Medina) you have no staying in it ... Husayn is killed thus my tears are overflowing

etymology.text: Noun of place from the verb قَامَ (qāma, “to get up”), from the root ق و م (q w m). Cognate with Hebrew מָקוֹם (māqōm) and Phoenician 𐤌𐤒𐤌 (mqm).
